raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to formally address a persistent issue regarding unauthorized charges on my Citi credit card ending in XXXX that requires urgent resolution. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, a fraudulent charge of {$1100.00} was made for " XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX. '' I promptly reported this to Citi, and the amount was credited back to my account on XX/XX/XXXX. However, this credit was reversed on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Despite this, in a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX, Citi provided a copy of the merchants response, which is essentially a duplicate of the transaction or use details : the purchase of XXXX XXXX XXXX tickets at XXXX XXXX in XXXX, MA ( order number XXXX, purchased on XX/XX/XXXX, at XXXX XXXX ), with merchant contact XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at XXXX. 

Citi has since closed my original credit card ending in XXXX and issued a new card ending in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Despite my repeated effortsincluding multiple contacts in XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX XXXX has yet to uphold its " {$0.00} Liability on Unauthorized Charges '' policy. The disputed charge remains unresolved, and the reversal of the credit is highly concerning. 

As a long-time cardholder, I trusted Citi to honor its commitment to protecting me from unauthorized transactions. This charge is unauthorized uses of our credit card because they were made by a person who does not have actual, implied, or apparent authority to use the credit card, and we, the cardholders, have received no benefit from the usage. In accordance with the Fair Credit Billing Act, 1026.12, our liability shall not exceed {$50.00} for this or any unauthorized use of our credit card.

The information and evidence provided hereafter shows 1 ) the charges themselves are suspicious and should never have been allowed by Citi Bank ; 2 ) we did not authorize the charges ; and 3 ) Citi Banks reasoning for considering the charges valid are flawed. 

Thank you for your immediate attention.
